Small Scarab Helm  
 


Slot: HEAD
AC: 4
CHA: -1
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST
Race: DWF HFL GNM FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Small Scarab Helm

Slot: HEAD
AC: 4
CHA: -1
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST
Race: DWF HFL GNM FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Plate
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):15, 66, 40
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:1 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:6
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 02:28:09
Page Updated:Sat May 30th, 2020

Average Price: 1pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 8

Expansion List - Premium only.

Scarab Helm
Scrap Metal

# Apr 05 2002 at 8:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777604590
5 posts
it looks like a turtles shell in inventory and is a green plate helm on you
▲ Page top
WHAT???
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2001 at 1:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570663612
23 posts
Wh7y is this monk usabale when humans or iksars can't use???
▲ Page top
WHAT???
QuoteReply
# Mar 10 2016 at 4:24 PM Rating: Good
animekenji
1,123 posts
It's flagged for all classes that can wear leather (at least the classes that could wear leather at the time the item was added to the game) and all races that wear small only. It's probably a requirement of the game engine that they do it that way even if some of the races and classes don't go together.

Edited, Mar 10th 2016 5:25pm by animekenji

Edited, Mar 10th 2016 5:25pm by animekenji
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
WHAT???
QuoteReply
# Mar 18 2016 at 6:57 AM Rating: Excellent
snailish
4,580 posts
animekenji wrote:
It's flagged for all classes that can wear leather (at least the classes that could wear leather at the time the item was added to the game) and all races that wear small only. It's probably a requirement of the game engine that they do it that way even if some of the races and classes don't go together.

Edited, Mar 10th 2016 5:25pm by animekenji

Edited, Mar 10th 2016 5:25pm by animekenji



There's many examples of what animekenji is talking about in the earlier content of the game. The devs were far more fixated on small/med/large races having to get different drops. This was abandoned and most of the time it has been by silk/leather/chain/plate divisions ever since. Much less programming, though at times far less interesting as well.

As far as this item goes, froglok monks now exist so they could use it. However, since they aren't unlocked until a way later era in the game (say on a progression server) it's unlikely it gets used.
▲ Page top
RE: WHAT???

Maybe one day, all the races can be all the classes....

just wondering if this looks like a plate helm
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 09 2001 at 12:12 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) bards can be half elfs
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 11:42 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) HFL is halfling man, half elf is HLF
RE: classes
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2001 at 1:21 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593971708573
View User Forum
194 posts
Actually, Half Elf is HEF
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 26 2001 at 10:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Uh there are no Monks, Shaman, ShadowKhights and Bards in these classes. Something is wierd here.
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 14 2001 at 8:14 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) To those that don't know about programing it looks like a *****-up, to those that do know it's just a "cut n paste" coding short-cut
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 26 2001 at 4:59 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hey moron, you dont need to be a programmer to know what "cut n paste" is, its used everywhere, not just for programming. I dont care if the programmer was trying to use a short cut, this IS a *****-up.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 07 2001 at 3:47 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Actually what he means by "cut n paste" is the program uses a filter that assignes what class and race an item can by due to matching critiera. This item matches the Criteria to be worn by War, Clr, Pal, Rng, Shd, Dru, Mnk, Brd, Rog, Shm. Then it looks for the size of the item and lits the appropriate races Dwf, Hfl, Gnm. By codeing it this way it allows them to make items in a quarter of the time by only having to put in statistics and size instead of havning to hand code what race/class it can be for each and every item in the game. But if you feel the need to complain over such trivial facts be my guest. :)
COOL!!!!!!!!
QuoteReply
# Feb 12 2001 at 1:25 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Badass! i cant wait to see my gnomish monk wearing a plate hat.. oo oo how about my halfling bard! yeah! and my dwarvish shaman.... :\ you'd think they'd have this whole race/class thing figured out by now...

#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 18 2001 at 7:30 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ROFL, yea, they should really check that before making the items. makes em look dumb. provides nice comic relief though *laughs at verant*
